description: Classes are pulled in with require/include by path, creating fragile load ordering and duplicate-definition errors.
body: Failure mode: code loads classes with require_once '../src/Foo.php' instead of relying on Composer's PSR-4 autoloader, so adding files or reordering includes causes class-not-found or cannot-redeclare errors and the namespace map is bypassed. Detection signals: require/include of class files in application code; fatal redeclare errors; a new class not found until a require is added by hand. Do not hand-manage class loading. Safer fallback: declare the namespace under autoload.psr-4 in composer.json, name files to match their class, run composer dump-autoload, and let the autoloader resolve classes lazily with a test confirming it.
